summary: Iteration 5 — Phase 3 (Market Sell tax tiers) + Phase 4 (Withdrawals + Admin) backend e2e + frontend visual smoke for PixelHero/MonsterSprite/kill-counter + admin tab visibility gating. Created /app/backend/tests/test_ton_economy_phase34.py with 21 pytest cases — ALL 21 PASSING (100%). Verified market_sell tax curve (vip=0→20%, vip=1→10%, vip=5→10%, vip=20→8%, vip=30→5%) plus inventory removal + gold credit + market_sales audit row; invalid inv_index → 400; unknown wallet → 404. Verified withdraw flow: atomic ton_balance debit on request (10→6.5 after 3.5 TON withdrawal), pending status row created, history sorted desc; insufficient balance → 400; unknown wallet → 404. Admin: /api/admin/check/6170049742→true, others→false; admin/withdrawals lists pending and 403s non-admins; approve sets status+admin_id+tx_hash+admin_note+decided_at and does NOT refund; reject refunds ton_balance atomically and 403s non-admins; double-approve → 400; not-found → 404. Frontend: ?guest=1 renders battle-arena with arena-hero (SVG PixelHero — blue tunic visible), arena-monster-<id> (SVG pixel-art wolf — confirmed contains <svg> tag, no emoji), kill-counter '0/1 kills' top-right; tab-admin correctly HIDDEN for guest:local wallet. Console: only benign Telegram WebApp HapticFeedback warnings (expected in non-TG preview) and TonConnect bridge CORS noise from external nicegram bridge (3rd-party, not our backend).

minor.issue: No upper bound on inv_index beyond '< len(inv)' and no validation that sell_price is reasonable vs item value — anyone with a wallet string can sell an item for an arbitrary self-declared price and mint gold (e.g., sell_price=10_000_000 → +8M gold credited). Server should compute sell_price from a price table, not trust client input.
No authentication — anyone who knows a wallet string can drain another player's inventory.

No auth — any party who knows a wallet string can submit a withdraw to any to_address; admins approval is the only safety. Should require Telegram initData or session token. to_address validation is also weak (min_length 10) — does not verify TON address checksum/format.

admin_id is taken from the request body and not cryptographically tied to a session — anyone who knows an admin Telegram ID can act as admin. Gate with Telegram initData verification (verify_telegram_init_data) and assert the verified user.id ∈ ADMIN_TELEGRAM_IDS.

Refund uses a non-conditional $inc — if the document is somehow already-rejected (race with another admin), the second call returns 400 BUT only after a refund was issued by the first call; ordering of update_one(withdrawals) vs $inc(players) matters. Currently: refund happens BEFORE withdrawals.update_one(status='rejected') status guard. Reverse the order: flip status first (conditional on status='pending'); only on modified_count==1 do the $inc refund. Otherwise concurrent admin clicks could double-refund.
No auth — any wallet's withdrawal history is publicly readable by anyone who knows the wallet string. Privacy / wallet-fingerprinting risk.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot be exercised in preview env without a real Telegram initData or TON Connect connection. The store (window.useGame) is not exposed globally, so playwright cannot simulate game.wallet='tg:6170049742'. Recommend exposing window.useGame in dev for testability, or providing a debug query param like ?debug_wallet=tg:6170049742.

wallet-withdraw-button only renders when a TON Connect wallet is connected (WalletPanel.jsx L95 returns connect button if !wallet). Cannot connect a real TON wallet in headless playwright. Backend POST /api/withdraw/request was fully exercised via pytest instead.

issues: Hero pixel-art renders with crisp blue tunic + red details + sword + shield; matches spec. Background = forest map (green tile field), not the old photo. Kill counter '0/1 kills' visible top-right. Monster SVG wolf rendered correctly. No visual regressions observed.

action_items: CRITICAL: Add server-side price oracle for /api/market/sell — never trust client-supplied sell_price (gold-mint exploit).
CRITICAL: Gate admin endpoints with verified Telegram initData, not body.admin_id (impersonation).
HIGH: Add Telegram initData / session auth to /api/withdraw/request, /api/market/sell, /api/withdrawals/{wallet}.
HIGH: In admin_reject_withdrawal, flip the withdrawal status update BEFORE the $inc refund and condition the $inc on modified_count==1 to prevent double-refund races.
MEDIUM: Validate to_address as a TON address (EQ/UQ prefix + base64url + checksum).
LOW: Expose window.useGame (zustand store) in dev mode for E2E testability of admin tab and store-dependent UI.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/market/sell uses int() truncation (Math.floor) for net_gold which is fine for gold but creates a tax_gold that always rounds UP — tax_gold = sell_price - floor(net) ≥ sell_price*tax_pct/100. Acceptable but document the rounding policy.
vip_level tax tiers in market_sell duplicate the logic in vip_benefits(level).market_tax_pct — pull from vip_benefits() to keep a single source of truth.
WithdrawRequest.to_address has min_length=10/max_length=128 but no format/checksum check — a typo will result in a permanently-lost withdrawal if approved.
AdminActionRequest.admin_id is a plain int from request body — trivially spoofable. Replace with verified Telegram initData; do not accept admin_id from the client.
admin_reject_withdrawal performs refund $inc on players BEFORE the conditional status update on withdrawals — re-order so status flips atomically first and only on success do you refund.
Audit collections market_sales / withdrawals lack indexes on wallet, status, created_at. Add for query performance.
Frontend gameStore is not exposed on window — hinders E2E testing. Consider window.useGame = useGame in dev only.

success_rate.frontend: 100% smoke (PixelHero SVG, MonsterSprite SVG, kill-counter, admin tab hidden for guest all verified). Admin-visible flow and withdraw dialog flow could not be e2e tested (TON Connect / Telegram auth required) — covered by backend pytest and code review.

test_credentials: No auth. Test wallets TEST_p34_<uuid8>. Admin ID 6170049742 from /app/backend/.env ADMIN_TELEGRAM_IDS.
seed_data_creation: Test wallets auto-created via GET /api/player/<wallet>, then ton_balance / vip_level / inventory set directly via Mongo. Cleaned up in module teardown across players, market_sales, withdrawals collections matching ^TEST_p34.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1–4 backend now fully covered: /app/backend/tests/test_ton_economy_phase12.py (20 tests, deposits+packs+VIP) and /app/backend/tests/test_ton_economy_phase34.py (21 tests, market+withdraw+admin) — total 41 passing. Helpers: _new_wallet('TEST_p34') for unique scoping; _seed_player(s, mongo, wallet, ton_balance=, vip_level=, inventory=) to bypass on-chain deposit. Admin ID hard-coded to 6170049742. For phase 5+, follow the same fixture pattern (s for HTTP session, mongo for direct DB writes, module-scope autouse cleanup). To E2E-test admin tab and withdraw button on frontend, request main agent to expose window.useGame in dev mode OR add a ?debug_wallet= query handler to GamePanel useEffect.
rca of the issue: N/A — all Phase 3/4 acceptance criteria pass. Reported items are NOT bugs from the request but defense-in-depth recommendations (auth gating, price oracle, refund ordering) the main agent should plan for the hardening phase.
